freshness_caveat: Coal data stale as of 2025-12-26 (132 days old) and Clean Dark as of 2025-12-31 (127 days old); merit-order and thermal dispatch assumptions may not reflect current fuel costs. All other metrics current as of 2026-05-06/07.
risk_flags: Hormuz closure since late February tightening LNG to EU; TTF at 57th percentile but upstream pressure via Asian LNG spreads may not yet be reflected in current snapshot.
EU storage at 34.26% (11th percentile, 12.4 pp below seasonal) — injecting season now tight; any supply disruption prolongs deficit into winter.
Clean Spark at 32.99 EUR/MWh (90th percentile) signals sustained gas-in-the-money regime; coal at 8th percentile (96 USD/t) leaves no coal upside cushion.
Coal data stale as of 2025-12-26 (132 days old); Clean Dark stale as of 2025-12-31 (127 days old) — merit-order curves may have shifted.
themes: Gas tightness via geopolitical LNG disruption
Thermal merit-order inversion: coal structurally weak, gas-fired spark spreading
EU storage refill race against seasonal deficit
top_drivers: Hormuz LNG blockade lifts gas-to-EU risk premium; Clean Spark at 90th percentile reflects sustained thermal-fuel-switch bias despite modest TTF level (57th percentile).
Coal structurally crushed (8th percentile); merit-order flattens toward gas, amplifying any LNG supply shock.
EU storage at 11th percentile, 12.4 pp below seasonal — refill season is race against clock; geopolitical LNG tightness directly threatens H2 adequacy.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 90th percentile amid Hormuz-driven LNG premium; EU storage 12.4 pp below seasonal average sets tight H2 refill risk.

watchlist.why: Closure since late February is the primary bullish LNG/TTF driver; restoration timeline will set near-term gas risk direction and EU storage refill pace.
17.5M barrels released since March suppresses Brent; end of program could rebound oil, tighten LNG arb feedback, and lift EU gas via crude anchor.
At 34.26% (11th percentile), 12.4 pp below seasonal — refill pace will define H2 margin; any supply shock halts progress and extends winter risk.

watchlist.metric: DE Power vs. GB Power spread
watchlist.why: DE at 132.57 EUR/MWh (74th percentile), GB at 111.78 (84th percentile); GB premium narrows (−8.29% daily) — watch for fuel-mix or grid-constraint repricing.
